channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: AI Talent: The 2025 Miss  #ai #artificialintelligence #jobs #2026 #aistrategy
executive_summary: In the video, Nate B Jones outlines five key trends shaping AI strategy for 2026, emphasizing the need for builders to plan now amidst the rapidly changing landscape. He highlights the importance of governance, CFO scrutiny, competition from AI-native companies, and the implications of local LLMs. Teams that invest in evaluation processes and vertical expertise will be better positioned to manage risks and seize opportunities as they arise.
bullets: Enforcement of regulations is turning governance into a builder’s market opportunity, meaning that companies can leverage compliance as a competitive edge. This is like finding a hidden treasure map; those who understand the rules can navigate better than those who don’t.

CFO scrutiny is pushing for outcome-based pricing for AI agents, which means companies will pay based on the results delivered rather than just for the technology itself. Think of it like paying for a meal at a restaurant based on how delicious it is, not just for the ingredients used.

AI-native entrants are outpacing incumbents, indicating that newer companies built on AI foundations are gaining an advantage over older, established firms. This is similar to a new athlete using the latest training techniques to outperform seasoned competitors who stick to outdated methods.

Local LLMs (Large Language Models) are becoming crucial, as they allow companies to tailor AI solutions to specific markets. Imagine a chef creating a dish that caters to local tastes rather than serving a one-size-fits-all meal.

Teams that invest now in evaluations will be better prepared for the challenges of 2026, which means they should assess their AI tools rigorously. This is like a coach reviewing game footage to improve strategies before the next match.

Multi-model resilience is essential for AI systems, meaning that using various models can enhance reliability and performance. Think of it like a band that plays different instruments to create a richer sound rather than relying on just one.

Agents on golden workflows are a priority, indicating that AI should be integrated into the most valuable processes within a business. It’s like ensuring that the most skilled workers are assigned to the most critical tasks for maximum efficiency.

Vertical expertise is becoming increasingly important, suggesting that teams should specialize in specific industries or sectors. This is like a doctor who focuses on one field of medicine, gaining deeper knowledge and skills than a general practitioner.

The timeline for planning is now, as companies need to start preparing for 2026 immediately. If they wait, they risk falling behind competitors who are already strategizing, much like a student who starts studying for exams weeks in advance versus one who crams the night before.

Investment in AI strategies now can help capture the upside in 2026, meaning that early adopters will benefit more than those who delay. This is like planting seeds in the spring to reap a harvest in the fall; the sooner you plant, the better your yield.

The speaker emphasizes that the AI hype cycle is constantly changing, making it crucial for builders to focus on real trends rather than fads. It’s like trying to catch a wave; if you wait too long, you’ll miss the ride.

The video suggests that there are five key drivers impacting AI strategy for 2026, which builders must understand to navigate the landscape effectively. This is akin to a sailor needing to know the five major winds that will affect their journey.
key_takeaways: Start planning your AI strategy for 2026 immediately to stay competitive. If you delay, you risk being outpaced by companies that are already investing in their future. Think of it like waiting too long to book a flight; prices will only go up and availability will decrease.

Ensure that at least 50% of your evaluation tests focus on code hygiene, such as naming conventions and structure, rather than just functionality. Most teams currently have this split backwards at 80/20, which can lead to messy code and increased maintenance costs.

Invest in multi-model resilience now to enhance the reliability of your AI systems. If you don’t, you risk creating a single point of failure that could cripple your operations, much like relying on one engine to power an entire airplane.

technical_terms.Governance: The framework of rules and practices that ensure accountability and transparency in AI development. Think of it like the rules of a game that everyone must follow to ensure fair play.
technical_terms.Outcome-based pricing: A pricing model where payment is based on the results achieved rather than the technology itself. Like paying for a successful marketing campaign based on the sales it generates, not just the ads created.
technical_terms.AI-native entrants: New companies that are built from the ground up using AI technologies. Think of them as startups that are like digital natives in a world of analog businesses.
technical_terms.Local LLMs: Large Language Models that are tailored to specific local markets or languages. Like a translator who specializes in regional dialects, making communication more effective.
technical_terms.Multi-model resilience: The strategy of using multiple AI models to ensure better performance and reliability. Think of it like having backup generators in case the main power source fails.
technical_terms.Golden workflows: The most valuable and efficient processes within a business where AI can be applied. Like a factory assembly line that maximizes output with minimal waste.
technical_terms.Vertical expertise: Specialized knowledge in a specific industry or sector that enhances the effectiveness of AI solutions. Like a mechanic who knows every detail of a specific car model, leading to better repairs.
technical_terms.AI hype cycle: The pattern of excitement and disillusionment that often surrounds new technologies. Think of it like a roller coaster ride, with thrilling highs and stomach-churning lows.
technical_terms.Evaluation tests: Assessments used to measure the effectiveness and quality of AI systems. Like a teacher grading students to determine their understanding of a subject.
technical_terms.CFO scrutiny: The careful examination by Chief Financial Officers regarding the financial implications of AI investments. Like a detective looking for clues to ensure every dollar spent is justified.
